data.description:
## Why
Deletion/asymmetric-change review is a positive analysis task the reviewer won't do unless prompted,
but it doesn't belong in the always-on base prompt. rebar's grounding oracle has no reverse-reference
lookup, so a purely deterministic version is imprecise new work. The aligned design is a specialist
LLM overlay gated by a deterministic diff-content trigger.

## What
- New capability: content-triggered overlays — a deterministic diff-content predicate that activates
  an overlay, alongside today's path-glob triggers (registry.glob_triggered_overlays seam).
- New deletion-impact overlay (agentic, findings-only, one-hop): triggered when the diff removes a
  def/class/signature (polyglot regex over '-' lines); searches for surviving callers/references of
  deleted/renamed/re-signatured symbols and flags dangling ones. Advisory/coach-not-block; claims flow
  through Pass-2 verify (grep/AST the specific caller -> confirm broken) and Pass-3 decide. Over-firing
  the trigger costs one advisory pass, never a wrong verdict.

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py (trigger seam), gates/code-review.yaml wiring,
src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-deletion-impact.md, criteria_routing.json entry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Content-trigger seam added and unit-tested; path-glob triggers unaffected.
- [ ] deletion-impact overlay fires on removed def/class/signature lines, not on unrelated diffs.
- [ ] Dangling-deletion recall eval passes; a valid rename-with-callers-updated does not fire.
- [ ] Overlay is advisory (no new BLOCK source).
data.title: Content-triggered overlay capability + deletion-impact overlay
